pages.raw_text:
From the best information OBJ
tainable the rumor that there had
been a shaking up" of some Of
the officials of the Washington
Water Power company was not al
together unfounded. The "shak-
ing up" was not what the public
thought 1t was and was confined
to one or two of the principal Of
ficers of the company. 1t has leak.
ed out that lively row took
place between General Manager
Huntington and superintendent
Lumsden over the placing of no.
tees in the cars asking the pub
lie to act as "spotters" for the
local monopoly and report any in.
fraction of certain rules by EN-.
ployes. The posting of the notices
was bitterly resented by the men
who claimed it was unjust as it
gave anyone who might have fan
cied a grievance against an en-
ploye the right to secure his dis
charge by reporting him to the
officers of the company. Superin-
tendent Lumsden is said to have
demonstrated with General Mana.
ger Huntington in a manner that
led to blows. The general mana
ger is said to have come out of
the melee considerably the worse
for wear and tear. The action of
Lumsden has increased his popu.
larity with the employees of the
company and the public generally.
The rumor that Mr.. Huntington
had resigned seems to be nothing
more than that he got licked.


Lumsden still holds his job, ac-
cording to last accounts. The car
notices have been removed.
